results.rendered_html:
puruṣakāra (pu˚ + 1. kāra) m.
1〉 die That des Menschen, menschliche Anstrengung (gewöhnlich im Gegens. zu daiva Schicksal): daive puruṣakāre ca karmasiddhirvyavasthitā  YĀJÑ. 1,348 .  MBH. 1,4778 . 3,12422 . 5,2826 . 7345 .  R. 1,58,24 .  Spr. 1251 . 1252 . 1682 . 1817 . 2315 . 2330 .  KATHĀS. 36,96 . kṛte puruṣakāre hi maraṇaṃ nābhibādhate  R. 4,61,59 . prajñāpuruṣakārābhyāṃ yuktasyāyatnena kāryasiddhayaḥ saṃbhavanti  PAÑCAT. 201,14 .  KIR. 5,52 . kathaṃ puruṣakāraḥ syāttu puruṣāṇāṃ mayi sthite  R. 2,23,36 .  BHĀG. P. 5,1,36 . naṣṭaṃ vinaṣṭaṃ kṛmibhiḥ śvahataṃ viṣame mṛtam . hīnaṃ puruṣakāreṇa pradadyātpāla eva tu .. worum sich kein Mensch gekümmert hat  M. 8,232 . ˚phala  VYUTP. 64 .
— 2〉 N. pr. eines Grammatikers  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. II, 48 .  WESTERGAARD, Radices II. fg.
